page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, supported by a cache hierarchy consisting of 64 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes a bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and compression capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 36.48 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 17.31 GB/sec, alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score of 4380.5. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong at 257.74 GB/sec. Standard compute benchmarks, such as stress-ng single-core and multi-core, fall into the average tier. Database operations show strong performance with a PassMark database score of 29397.82, and Redis SET operations average 48.58 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARMv9 architecture combined with high-density local NVMe SSD storage offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring fast local scratch space without the overhead of network-attached storage. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, database operations, and medium-sized language model inference, where it achieves a strong 29.71 tokens per second for 7B model text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, a 950 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and an 8 Gbps baseline network speed, running on the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks reveal top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and PassMark memory tests, alongside strong results in static web serving and database operations. While standard CPU compute benchmarks like stress-ng fall in the average tier, its dedicated ARM64 cores and high-speed local storage provide a highly efficient, resource-dense platform. This instance is well-suited for database hosting, web serving, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 950 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering strong database and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 16 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance combining dedicated Graviton processors with high-speed local NVMe storage for balanced, memory-efficient workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression benchmarks
Strong throughput for static web serving and database operations
Optimized for general-purpose applications, database hosting, and web servers
